concerns.analysis: The source document includes an official state seal at the top of page 1 and the North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) logo at the bottom of page 1. The HTML output lacks corresponding `` elements for these distinct, meaningful agency identifiers in their correct structural locations (there is no image at the bottom of the page 1 content).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: NC DEQ logo at the bottom left of page 1.
concerns.output_quote: Missing corresponding `` element at the end of the first page's text content.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output shows clear visual duplication. On page 2, the text regarding the mailing date is transcribed as a paragraph and then immediately duplicated inside an image snippet. Additionally, the HTML includes large image blocks (e.g., alt="A formal business letter...") embedded directly alongside the fully transcribed text of the document, resulting in multiple full-page or large-block text redundancies.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The text "This notice was mailed on 12-15-2020" and the signature block on page 2.
concerns.output_quote:
`

This notice was mailed on 12-15-2020

` followed by ``

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 5 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=5. Examples: p1: "A formal business letter regarding a revised Notic"; p1: "A typed letter from David Miller, PE, State Mining"; p1: "A formal letter from State Mining Engineer David M". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 5 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (5 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...d Notice of Issuance of Mining Permit for Alamance"; p1: "... State Mining Engineer, regarding a revised Notice"; p1: "... a revised Notice of Issuance of Mining Permit for"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=5 (truncated=5, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.
The source document includes underlined text for emphasis, specifically the phrase "within sixty (60) days" on page 2. This underline emphasis was lost in the JSON output.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
